---
title: "Global Language Diversity on Wikipedia"
description: "A table showing the relationship between language speaker populations and Wikipedia article counts."
author: "Carwil Bjork-James"
date: "February 23, 2026"
format: 
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
---

# Languages and Wikipedia Articles

![The largest Wikipedia editions by number of articles, 2024 (CC-0 [Arief Azazie Zain](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/User:Arief_Azazie_Zain "User:Arief Azazie Zain")) ](/images/wikipedia/List_of_Wikipedia_articles_by_language_-_March_2024.svg.png){alt="The largest Wikipedia editions by number of articles"}

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(rvest)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(stringi)
library(viridis)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)

linguameta_root_path <- "~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/url-nlp/linguameta"
```

```{r load-data, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load linguameta
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
linguameta <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path(linguameta_root_path, "linguameta.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Scrape live Wikipedia table and clean column names
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
wiki_html <- read_html("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias")

wiki <- wiki_html |>
  html_table() |>
  keep(~ "Articles" %in% names(.)) |>
  _[[1]] |>
  rename(
    wiki_name_en     = `Wikipedia name in English`,
    wiki_name_native = `Wikipedia namein native language`,
    language         = Language,
    script           = `Script (ISO15924 code)`,
    wp_code          = WPcode,
    articles         = Articles,
    active_users     = Activeusers,
    launch_date      = `Launch date`
  ) |>
  mutate(
    articles     = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", articles)),
    active_users = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", active_users))
  ) |>
  filter(articles > 0) |>
  # Wikipedia edition link: English name + native name
  # For macrolanguage members, use "In [Macro]" prefix; otherwise just the name
  mutate(
    wp_edition_display = paste0(
      '<a href="https://', wp_code, '.wikipedia.org/">',
      wiki_name_en,
      '</a>',
      if_else(!is.na(wiki_name_native) & wiki_name_native != "", 
              # Insert line break before any parentheses in endonym
              paste0(' <br><em>', 
                     str_replace(wiki_name_native, '\\s*\\(', '<br>\\('),
                     '</em>'),
              '')
    )
  )

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Join wiki → linguameta via BCP-47 code
#    Some Wikipedia edition codes are non-standard (zh-yue, zh-min-nan,
#    or carry "(closed)" suffixes); map them to canonical BCP-47 tags.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
wp_to_bcp47_extra <- c(
  # Non-standard Wikipedia codes: map linguameta codes TO the Wikipedia's non-standard code
  # (Wikipedia uses zh-yue, zh-min-nan etc.; linguameta uses the clean BCP-47 form)
  "yue"     = "zh-yue",     # Yue Chinese / Cantonese → Cantonese Wikipedia
  "nan"     = "zh-min-nan", # Min Nan Chinese → Southern Min Wikipedia
  # Codes that are identical in linguameta and Wikipedia but non-standard in WP
  "cr"      = "cr",    # Cree
  "kl"      = "kl",    # Kalaallisut / Greenlandic
  "kr"      = "knc",   # Kanuri → Central Kanuri Wikipedia (WP uses knc, not kr)
  "aa"      = "aa",    # Afar
  "ii"      = "ii",    # Sichuan Yi
  "ho"      = "ho",    # Hiri Motu
  "hz"      = "hz",    # Herero
  "kj"      = "kj",    # Kuanyama
  "mh"      = "mh",    # Marshallese
  "na"      = "na",    # Nauruan
  "ng"      = "ng",    # Ndonga
  "lrc"     = "lrc",   # Northern Luri
  # Chinese variants → specific or general Chinese Wikipedia
  "cmn"     = "zh",    # Mandarin Chinese → Chinese Wikipedia
  "cjy"     = "zh",    # Jinyu Chinese → Chinese Wikipedia
  "hsn"     = "zh",    # Xiang Chinese → Chinese Wikipedia
  "czh"     = "zh",    # Huizhou Chinese → Chinese Wikipedia
  # Arabic dialects → Arabic Wikipedia
  "arz"     = "ar",    # Egyptian Arabic
  "arq"     = "ar",    # Algerian Arabic
  "apd"     = "ar",    # Sudanese Arabic
  "aec"     = "ar",    # Saidi Arabic
  "acm"     = "ar",    # Mesopotamian Arabic
  "acw"     = "ar",    # Hijazi Arabic
  "acx"     = "ar",    # Omani Arabic
  "ayp"     = "ar",    # North Mesopotamian Arabic
  "ayh"     = "ar",    # Hadrami Arabic
  "ayl"     = "ar",    # Libyan Arabic
  "ayn"     = "ar",    # Sanaani Arabic
  "abh"     = "ar",    # Tajiki Arabic
  "acy"     = "ar",    # Cypriot Arabic
  "acq"     = "ar",    # Ta'Izzi-Adeni Arabic
  # Other macrolanguages & dialect variants
  "azj"     = "az",    # North Azerbaijani → Azerbaijani Wikipedia
  "kpv"     = "kv",    # Komi-Zyrian → Komi Wikipedia (FIX: was incorrectly mapped to ku)
  "sdh"     = "ku",    # Southern Kurdish → Kurdish Wikipedia
  "nb"      = "no",    # Norwegian Bokmål → Norwegian Wikipedia
  "fil"     = "tl",    # Filipino → Tagalog Wikipedia
  "bho"     = "bh",    # Bhojpuri → Bhojpuri Wikipedia
  "bik"     = "bcl",   # Bikol → Central Bikol Wikipedia
  "prs"     = "fa",    # Dari → Persian Wikipedia
  "fuv"     = "ff",    # Nigerian Fulfulde → Fula Wikipedia
  "fuc"     = "ff",    # Pulaar → Fula Wikipedia
  "kng"     = "kg",    # Koongo → Kongo Wikipedia
  "gug"     = "gn",    # Paraguayan Guaraní → Guaraní Wikipedia
  "hno"     = "pnb",   # Northern Hindko → W. Punjabi (via Lahnda macro)
  "jvn"     = "jv",    # Caribbean Javanese → Javanese Wikipedia
  "ory"     = "or",    # Odia → Odia Wikipedia
  "uzn"     = "uz",    # Northern Uzbek → Uzbek Wikipedia
  "uzs"     = "uz",    # Southern Uzbek → Uzbek Wikipedia
  "npi"     = "ne",    # Nepali → Nepali Wikipedia
  "pbt"     = "ps",    # Southern Pashto → Pashto Wikipedia
  "pst"     = "ps",    # Central Pashto → Pashto Wikipedia
  "pes"     = "fa",    # Iranian Persian → Persian Wikipedia
  "ak"      = "tw",    # Twi → Twi Wikipedia
  "swc"     = "sw",    # Congo Swahili → Swahili Wikipedia
  "nod"     = "th",    # Northern Thai → Thai Wikipedia
  "sou"     = "th",    # Southern Thai → Thai Wikipedia
  "tts"     = "th",    # Northeastern Thai → Thai Wikipedia
  "gsw"     = "de",    # Swiss German → German Wikipedia
  "dip"     = "din",   # Northeastern Dinka → Dinka Wikipedia
  "khk"     = "mn",    # Halh Mongolian → Mongolian Wikipedia
  "mvf"     = "mn",    # Peripheral Mongolian → Mongolian Wikipedia
  "hae"     = "om",    # Eastern Oromo → Oromo Wikipedia
  "gax"     = "om",    # Borana-Arsi-Guji Oromo → Oromo Wikipedia
  "aln"     = "sq",    # Gheg Albanian → Albanian Wikipedia
  "bvu"     = "ms",    # Bukit Malay → Malay Wikipedia
  "mfa"     = "ms",    # Pattani Malay → Malay Wikipedia
  "mui"     = "ms",    # Musi → Malay Wikipedia
  "xmv"     = "mg",    # Antankarana Malagasy → Malagasy Wikipedia
  "lah"     = "pnb",   # Lahnda (macrolanguage) → Western Punjabi Wikipedia
  "kok"     = "gom",   # Konkani → Goan Konkani Wikipedia
  "tzm"     = "zgh"    # Central Atlas Tamazight → Standard Moroccan Amazigh Wikipedia
)

wiki_joined <- wiki |>
  mutate(
    # strip " (closed)", " (deleted)" etc.
    wp_code_clean = str_trim(str_remove(wp_code, "\\s*\\(.*"))
  ) |>
  select(wp_code_clean, wp_code, articles, active_users, language, wiki_name_en, wiki_name_native, wp_edition_display)

# Wiki editions that don't map to any spoken language in linguameta
# (constructed languages, classical languages, Simple English, dialects
#  with non-standard codes, etc.)
wiki_special <- wiki |>
  mutate(
    wp_code_clean = str_trim(str_remove(wp_code, "\\s*\\(.*"))
  ) |>
  filter(!wp_code_clean %in% linguameta$bcp_47_code) |>
  select(language, wp_code, articles, active_users)

text_to_num <- function(text) {
  text <- str_replace_all(text, ",", "")
  multipliers <- c(k = 1e3, K = 1e3, M = 1e6, B = 1e9)
  pattern <- "([0-9.]+)\\s*([KMBk]?)"
  
  as.numeric(sapply(text, function(x) {
    matches <- str_match(x, pattern)
    if (is.na(matches[1,1])) {
      return(NA)
    }
    number <- as.numeric(matches[1,2])
    suffix <- matches[1,3]
    if (suffix != "") {
      number * multipliers[[suffix]]
    } else {
      number
    }
  }))
}
```

```{r join-data}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Supplementary speaker estimates for languages where linguameta
#    has NA (sourced from Wikipedia language articles)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
na_speakers_supplement <- tribble(
  ~bcp_47_code, ~speakers_supplement, ~speakers_source,
  "tl",   33000000,  "L1 speakers (Ethnologue 2023, via Wikipedia)",
  "crh",    580000,  "Native speakers (Ethnologue 2001, via Wikipedia)",
  "nrm",    100000,  "Approximate (Norman language, France/Channel Islands)",
  "bxr",    218000,  "Russia Buryat (Ethnologue estimate)",
  "szy",     10000,  "Sakizaya (estimated, Taiwan)",
  "rsk",    600000,  "Pannonian Rusyn/Ruthenian (approximate)",
  "bdr",    200000,  "West Coast Bajau (approximate)"
)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Attach Wikipedia data to linguameta; fill NA speakers; assign
#    global rank; then partition into three groups:
#      A. Top 250 by speakers
#      B. Smaller languages with a Wikipedia (outside top 250)
#      C. Special / constructed / classical Wikipedias (no linguameta row)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
linguameta_with_wiki <- linguameta |>
  # Transform linguameta codes using the mapping: if lah→pnb, look up pnb in Wikipedia
  mutate(
    bcp47_wiki_lookup = coalesce(wp_to_bcp47_extra[bcp_47_code], bcp_47_code)
  ) |>
  left_join(wiki_joined, by = c("bcp47_wiki_lookup" = "wp_code_clean")) |>
  left_join(na_speakers_supplement, by = "bcp_47_code") |>
  mutate(
    speakers = coalesce(estimated_number_of_speakers, speakers_supplement)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    global_rank = row_number(desc(coalesce(speakers, 0L)))
  ) |>
  filter(global_rank <= 250 | !is.na(articles)) |>
  mutate(articles = replace_na(articles, 0))

# Partition
top250   <- linguameta_with_wiki |> 
  filter(global_rank <= 250) |> 
  arrange(desc(speakers))
extras   <- linguameta_with_wiki |> 
  filter(global_rank > 250, articles > 0) |> 
  arrange(desc(speakers))
```


```{r formatting}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Helper: format a data frame for display
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
format_for_display <- function(df, show_rank = TRUE) {
  df |>
    mutate(
      rank_label = if (show_rank) as.character(global_rank) else "—",
      is_roman   = stri_detect_regex(
        endonym, "^[\\p{Script=Latin}[:punct:][:space:]0-9]+$"
      ),
      autonym_fmt = if_else(
        !is.na(endonym) & is_roman,
        paste0(" <i>", endonym, "</i>"),
        paste0(" ", replace_na(endonym, ""))
      ),
      language_display = paste0(rank_label, ". ", english_name, "<br>", autonym_fmt),
      articles_fmt     = format(articles, big.mark = ","),
      # Wikipedia edition: use mapping if available; add "In" prefix only if mapped via macrolanguage
      # and if the language doesn't have its own Wikipedia (articles originally == 0)
      wiki_edition_fmt = if_else(
        !is.na(wp_edition_display),
        # Check if this is a mapped entry (wp_code exists but original articles were 0)
        if_else(
          !is.na(wp_code) & bcp_47_code %in% c("cmn", "cjy", "hsn", "arz", "arq", "apd", "aec", 
                                               "acm", "acw", "acx", "ayp", "ayh", "ayl", "ayn", 
                                               "abh", "acy", "azj", "kpv"),
          paste0("In ", wp_edition_display),
          wp_edition_display
        ),
        "—"
      )
    )
}
```

```{r color-scale}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Colour scale helpers (shared domain across both tables)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
all_speakers <- linguameta_with_wiki$speakers
color_pal <- col_numeric(
  palette = c("#ffffff", "#74a9cf", "#0570b0"),
  domain  = log1p(c(all_speakers, 1000000, NA))
)

make_article_colors <- function(articles) {
  case_when(
    articles == 0          ~ "#ffffff",
    articles < 10000       ~ "#e6f7f0",
    articles < 100000      ~ "#bae4d7",
    articles < 1000000     ~ "#82c9a5",
    articles < 4000000     ~ "#c1d383",
    articles >= 4000000    ~ "#ffd700"
  )
}

render_table <- function(df) {
  fmt <- format_for_display(df, show_rank = TRUE)
  speaker_colors <- color_pal(log1p(fmt$speakers))
  article_colors <- make_article_colors(fmt$articles)
  
  # Format speakers as comma-separated numbers
  fmt <- fmt |>
    mutate(speakers_fmt = format(speakers, big.mark = ","))

  fmt |>
    select(language_display, wikidata_description, speakers_fmt, articles_fmt, wiki_edition_fmt) |>
    kable(
      format    = "html",
      escape    = FALSE,
      col.names = c("Language", "Language Description",
                    "Speakers (L1 est.)", "Wikipedia Articles", "Wikipedia Edition"),
      align     = "llrrr"
    ) |>
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
      font_size         = 14,
      htmltable_class   = "table table-hover table-condensed"
    ) |>
    column_spec(1, width = "25%") |>
    column_spec(2, width = "25%") |>
    column_spec(3, background = speaker_colors, width = "12%") |>
    column_spec(4, background = article_colors, width = "12%") |>
    column_spec(5, width = "26%") |>
    row_spec(0, background = "white", color = "black", bold = TRUE)
}
```

## Top 250 Languages by Number of Speakers

Speaker estimates are L1 (first-language) counts from [LinguaMeta](https://github.com/cisnlp/linguameta), supplemented for a handful of languages where LinguaMeta lacks an estimate (sourced from Wikipedia). Background shading: **blue** = speaker population; **green/gold** = Wikipedia article count.

:::{.column-page-inset-right}
```{r table-top250, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
render_table(top250)
```
:::

## Top 250 at a Glance

Languages ranked left-to-right, top-to-bottom by estimated L1 speaker population. Background color reflects Wikipedia article count: **slate** = no Wikipedia; **teal/green** = small–medium edition; **amber** = largest editions (1M+ articles).

:::{.column-page-inset-right}
```{r grid-top250, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(purrr)

fmt_compact <- function(n) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(n) | n == 0  ~ "\u2014",
    n >= 1e9            ~ sprintf("%.1fB", n / 1e9),
    n >= 1e6            ~ sprintf("%.1fM", n / 1e6),
    n >= 1e3            ~ sprintf("%.0fK", n / 1e3),
    TRUE                ~ as.character(round(n))
  )
}

square_bg <- function(articles) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    articles == 0        ~ "#414141",
    articles < 10000     ~ "#204a3a",
    articles < 100000    ~ "#14532d",
    articles < 1000000   ~ "#166534",
    articles < 4000000   ~ "#713f12",
    articles >= 4000000  ~ "#92400e"
  )
}

person_svg <- '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="11" height="11" fill="white" style="vertical-align:middle;margin-right:2px" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M8 8a3 3 0 1 0 0-6 3 3 0 0 0 0 6zm4 8c0 1-1 1-1 1H3s-1 0-1-1 1-4 6-4 6 3 6 4z"/></svg>'
page_svg  <- '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="11" height="11" fill="white" style="vertical-align:middle;margin-right:2px" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M14 4.5V14a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V2a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h5.5L14 4.5zm-3 0A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 9.5 3V1H4a1 1 0 0 0-1 1v12a1 1 0 0 0 1 1h8a1 1 0 0 0 1-1V4.5h-2z"/></svg>'

make_cell <- function(english_name, endonym, speakers, articles) {
  bg      <- square_bg(articles)
  end_html <- if (!is.na(endonym) && endonym != "")
    paste0('<br><span style="opacity:0.75;font-style:italic;font-size:10px">', endonym, '</span>')
  else ""
  sprintf(
    '<td style="background:%s;color:white;padding:5px 5px 4px 5px;vertical-align:top;font-size:11px;line-height:1.35;border:2px solid #1e1e1e"><strong style="font-size:11px">%s</strong>%s<br><span style="white-space:nowrap;font-size:10px">%s%s &nbsp;%s%s</span></td>',
    bg, english_name, end_html,
    person_svg, fmt_compact(speakers), 
    page_svg,   fmt_compact(articles)
  )
}

n_cols <- 8
grid_data <- top250 |> arrange(global_rank)
cells <- pmap_chr(
  list(grid_data$english_name, grid_data$endonym, grid_data$speakers, grid_data$articles),
  make_cell
)
while (length(cells) < 250) cells <- c(cells, '<td style="background:#1e1e1e;border:2px solid #1e1e1e"></td>')
cells <- cells[1:250]

html_rows <- map_chr(seq(1, 250, by = n_cols), function(i) {
  paste0('<tr>', paste(cells[i:min(i + n_cols - 1, 250)], collapse = ""), '</tr>')
})

cat(paste0(
  '<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;table-layout:fixed">',
  paste(html_rows, collapse = "\n"),
  '</table>'
))
```
:::

## Additional Languages with Wikipedia Editions

These languages fall outside the top 250 by speaker population but have active Wikipedia editions. Global rank (by estimated L1 speakers) shown where available.

:::{.column-page-inset-right}
```{r table-extras, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
extras_fmt <- format_for_display(extras, show_rank = TRUE)
speaker_colors_e <- color_pal(log1p(extras_fmt$speakers))
article_colors_e <- make_article_colors(extras_fmt$articles)

# Format speakers as comma-separated numbers
extras_fmt <- extras_fmt |>
  mutate(speakers_fmt = format(speakers, big.mark = ","))

extras_fmt |>
  select(language_display, wikidata_description, speakers_fmt, articles_fmt, wiki_edition_fmt) |> 
  kable(
    format    = "html",
    escape    = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Language", "Language Description",
                  "Speakers (L1 est.)", "Wikipedia Articles", "Wikipedia Edition"),
    align     = "llrrr"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
    font_size         = 14,
    htmltable_class   = "table table-hover table-condensed"
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "25%") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "25%") |>
  column_spec(3, background = speaker_colors_e, width = "12%") |>
  column_spec(4, background = article_colors_e, width = "12%") |>
  column_spec(5, width = "26%") |>
  row_spec(0, background = "white", color = "black", bold = TRUE)
```
:::

## Classical, Constructed, and Special-Purpose Wikipedia Editions

These Wikipedia editions are not associated with a living natural language in the LinguaMeta dataset. They include classical and liturgical languages (Latin, Sanskrit, Old English), constructed languages (Esperanto is covered above; Ido, Interlingua, Volapük, etc. appear here), dialect/regional-code editions, and the Simple English Wikipedia.

:::{.column-page-inset-right}
```{r table-special, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
article_colors_s <- make_article_colors(wiki_special$articles)

wiki_special |>
  mutate(articles_fmt = format(articles, big.mark = ",")) |>
  arrange(desc(articles)) |>
  select(language, wp_code, articles_fmt) |>
  kable(
    format    = "html",
    escape    = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Wikipedia Edition", "WP Code", "Articles"),
    align     = "llr"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
    font_size         = 14,
    htmltable_class   = "table table-hover table-condensed"
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "50%") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "20%") |>
  column_spec(3, background = article_colors_s, width = "30%") |>
  row_spec(0, background = "white", color = "black", bold = TRUE)
```
:::
